segments.text: Let's see how recursion works by tracing through factorial of four.
Here's the factorial function. It calls itself with a smaller number until it reaches the base case.
On the right, we'll build up the call stack.
First, factorial of four is called. It needs factorial of three to compute its answer, so that goes on the stack.
Factorial of three needs factorial of two.
And factorial of two needs factorial of one.
Factorial of one — this is the base case.
The base case returns one. Now the stack starts unwinding.
Factorial of two multiplies two times one, giving two.
Factorial of three multiplies three times two, giving six.
And factorial of four multiplies four times six.
The final answer is twenty-four. Each call waited for the one below it to finish — that's recursion in action.
